BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2016

PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT APPLICATION FORM
Please fill in and save this form in Word or ODS. The more information you
provide, the better I will be able to assist you. If you have any questions
with regards to this form, please do not hesitate to contact me. You can
use whichever measurement system (e.g. metric, imperial) you prefer to
fill in this form. Just state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

General
 Full legal name: John W.

● Age: 48

● Height: 6'2

● Weight: 185

● Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 20 scale

● Years of training experience: 0

Strength
Untrained

What are your fitness goals?

Body comp and strength increase

How often in a week would you be prepared to train, if this was
the optimal training frequency?

pictures of yourself in clear lighting, preferably including a back double biceps
and front relaxed pose.

Legal disclaimer: By returning this intake form, you declare that while Menno
Henselmans is your personal trainer, all the substances you use are obtained
and used in accordance with their governing laws and you forego all rights to
hold Menno Henselmans liable for any damages caused in relation to following
his advice as your trainer.

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2016

Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:

Here we have an untrained male insisting on a ketogenic diet. Due to him being new to
strength training and dieting, making long hours in the office and being nearly 50 years old,
compliance problems are likely, so I started him on a rather flexible ketogenic diet with a set
of rules rather than strict macros in each meal. He also had the idea that a ketogenic diet
was about stuffing yourself with fats, like butter, so I explicitly set up no minimum fat intake.

Note how there is no nutrient timing being implemented here yet at all except for the
prescription to have his meals at regular times to build a meal planning route and dietary
habits. I deemed this to be key during his first weeks.

Here are the guidelines I sent him. We also started monitoring postprandial blood ketone
levels.

In the first phase of your (ketogenic) diet, you only need to worry about a few
things.
1. You should be consuming 4 meals daily, ideally at regular times.
Please return the filled in form to
MennoHenselmans@BayesianBodybuilding.com and attach at least
two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without excessive
shadows; not your most flattering Facebook selfies), preferably
including a back double biceps and front relaxed pose. If you recently
had blood work done, please attach its results as well.
headings: In the first phase of your (ketogenic) diet, you only need to worry about a few

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2016

2. You should consume sufficient protein with each meal: each meal should contain a
minimum of 33 g of protein.
3. You should consume sufficient protein in each day: your total protein intake should
be 151 g or more every day.
4. Your total caloric intake should be no higher than 2078 kcal.
5. Your total daily net carb intake* should not exceed 50 g (but lower is not better:
getting in your vegetables is very important nutritionally).

You are highly recommended to fill your carbs with spinach, seaweed, avocado,
cauliflower, coconut, mushrooms, shirataki noodles and zucchini.
As protein sources, cottage cheese, meats, fish, eggs and poultry are good dietary
staples.
Parmesan cheese, cacao, garlic, commercial low carb pesto and commercial low carb
Italian sauce are great ways to add taste to your meals without adding tons of
calories.
*Net carb intake = total carb intake - fiber intake + 0.5 * excess protein intake
Excess protein is anything over the listed target.
Intuitively, you construct your meals by filling up your protein and net carb amounts
and then fill up the remaining calories with fats.
